name: Multi-Agent SOC — 9 Parallel AI Security Agents
description: APEX Multi-Agent SOC: 9 specialist AI agents (CVE Intel, IOC Hunter, SIEM Defender, Threat Hunter, IR Playbook, Compliance Guardian, Red Team, Zero Trust Sentinel, Risk Synthesizer) dispatched in parallel against a free-text task, with CVE/KEV/IOC context enrichment, real-time SSE streaming, and an executive risk-synthesis brief.

routes_sampled.evidence: workers/src/index.js:1955-1958 (path.startsWith prefix dispatch, agent_id read via path.split('/')[4])

frontend.evidence: frontend/soc-agents.html calls all 4 routes directly (fetch('/api/agents/stream') :427, fetch('/api/agents/run') :542, fetch('/api/agents/dispatch/...') :627,674, fetch('/api/agents/status') :699). frontend/index.html does NOT call any /api/agents/* route directly (0 matches) — it only links out to /soc-agents from the primary desktop nav (frontend/index.html:1142), mobile drawer (:1183), a homepage feature card (:5599-5609), and the flagship products grid (:5645). 6 other pages (frontend/mssp.html, frontend/god-mode.html, frontend/ai-red-team.html, frontend/soc-operations.html, frontend/attack-surface-management.html, frontend/threat-hunting.html, frontend/vulnerability-management.html) also link to /soc-agents; frontend/soc-dashboard.html and frontend/enterprise-portal.html embed only the GET /api/agents/status widget, not run/stream/dispatch.

navigation.evidence: Prominent placement in the primary desktop nav (frontend/index.html:1142, styled as a pill) and mobile drawer (:1183), plus a homepage feature card and 8 other product pages' cross-links. The /soc-agents target resolves via Cloudflare Pages' native Clean-URL lookup (frontend/_redirects, no explicit rewrite needed) to the real, present frontend/soc-agents.html — confirmed not a dead CTA.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/multiAgentSOC.test.mjs:32-40 imports classifyTask, fetchCVEContext, fetchKEVStatus, fetchIOCContext, runAgent, handleAgentsStatus, handleAgentsRun, handleAgentDispatch directly from workers/src/handlers/multiAgentSOC.js — 7 of 9 entry_points have real describe()-block coverage. handleAgentsStream (the SSE handler) is imported by nothing and has zero tests anywhere under workers/test/ (confirmed by grep). fetchIOCContext is imported at line 35 but never called in any it() block — imported, not exercised. workers/test/copilotAdvancedCapabilities.test.mjs:287-318 tests the AI-Copilot's use of this capability but does so against a full vi.doMock() of workers/src/handlers/multiAgentSOC.js, not the real module.

verification.evidence: FIXED this pass (dedicated change, own commit, own regression test — per docs/capability-registry/PROGRAM_BOARD.md's recommendation to not bundle it into a process/documentation PR): workers/src/index.js run/stream/dispatch route blocks (~1945-1968) now call `if (!isRealUser(authCtx)) return withSecurityHeaders(withCors(unauthorized(), request));` immediately after resolveAuthV5, using the identical established pattern already in place at 30+ other routes (e.g. the MFA block immediately above it) — no new middleware, no duplicated authorization logic. status is deliberately left ungated: it is read-only registry/health data with no compute cost, embedded unauthenticated in the public SOC dashboard widget (frontend/soc-dashboard.html:539, frontend/enterprise-portal.html), and both frontend consumers of the gated routes (frontend/soc-agents.html) already shipped with dormant 401-handling (handleUnauth(), :387-393, and per-call `if (resp.status===401)` checks at :434,544,628,675) that had never been exercised until now — this fix requires zero frontend changes. Regression coverage added: workers/test/authGateRealUser.test.mjs now asserts anonymous POST /api/agents/{run,stream,dispatch/:id} → 401, admin key → not-401 for run and dispatch, and anonymous GET /api/agents/status stays non-401. Full suite re-run green: 177 files / 1862 tests (npx vitest run, 2026-07-08). Prior static-verification evidence for the original finding retained below for audit trail.

notes: FIXED 2026-07-08 (see verification.evidence for the fix). Original finding, retained verbatim for audit trail: Highest-value finding: none of the 4 MASOC routes gate on isRealUser(authCtx) or check authCtx.authenticated before running — resolveAuthV5's IP-fallback branch sets authenticated:true for every anonymous caller (workers/src/auth/middleware.js's own comment: 'authenticated: true means not-an-invalid-API-key, NOT logged in'), and 30+ other routes in workers/src/index.js do gate on isRealUser (e.g. :2054,3358,4050) including the MFA block immediately above this one (:1917). The only real control on these 4 routes is a 5-runs/min KV rate limiter keyed by user_id/key_id/IP (workers/src/handlers/multiAgentSOC.js:176-199, fail-closed if KV is unavailable) — meaning anyone, unauthenticated, can invoke compute-expensive parallel AI-agent orchestration. This directly contradicts a SEPARATE, correctly-gated path to the same capability: workers/src/handlers/aiSecurityCopilot.js:1008,1861-1868,2718 exposes the same functionality as a Copilot tool restricted to PRO/TEAM/ENTERPRISE/MSSP tiers, actively enforced. subscription_gated is recorded as false here because it describes THIS capability's own 4 direct routes; run/stream/dispatch now require a real logged-in principal but still carry no tier/subscription gate of their own (unlike the Copilot-mediated path) — that remains a separate, unfixed gap, not conflated with the auth-gate fix. Two further confirmed-but-not-fixed bugs remain open (out of scope for this auth-gate fix — both touch either live CORS behavior or frontend state logic, judged too architecturally consequential to change without confirming intent): (1) handleAgentsStream (workers/src/handlers/multiAgentSOC.js:640-643) hand-rolls its own CORS check instead of using the shared withCors() the other 3 routes use, and its check is narrower than the real 6-origin PROD_ORIGINS allowlist (workers/src/middleware/cors.js) — 3 of 6 valid production origins (cyberdudebivash.pages.dev, tools.cyberdudebivash.com, intel.cyberdudebivash.com) would have SSE streaming silently rejected by the browser while the JSON/status routes keep working fine from the same origin. (2) frontend/soc-agents.html defaults to selecting all 9 agents including risk_synthesizer (:226-236,269) and both its run paths always send a non-empty agents array (:403,528), so the page's own label text ('all selected = auto-routing by task classifier', :157) is unreachable — classifyTask() is never invoked from this page — and because risk_synthesizer runs once standalone inside the parallel batch (with only CVE/KEV/IOC context, contradicting its own system prompt's expectation of other agents' findings) AND again correctly via the separate synthesis step, every default run burns an extra AI call on a low-value duplicate card. Minor/cosmetic: handleAgentsStatus (:697-702) checks only 4 of the 6 env keys workers/src/core/aiProviderRouter.js actually routes through (missing TOGETHER_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY), understating the 'X/4 providers active' stat — Groq/DeepSeek/CF AI (the 3 the UI copy names) are all genuinely called via real HTTP requests, confirmed in workers/src/core/aiProviderRouter.js.
